title: The Whip of Cords
subtitle: Christ drives the merchants from the temple, consumed with zeal for his Father's house

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the marketplace fall away.
God enters his Church each day in spirit, and watches how each one lives.
Let him find you quiet, not occupied with idle tales, or laughter, or hatreds, or greed.
gospel: The Passover of the Jews was near,
and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
And he found in the temple those selling oxen, and sheep, and doves,
And when he had made a kind of whip out of little cords,
he drove them all out of the temple, the sellers and the money-changers,
the sheep too, and the oxen,
and he poured out the coins of the money-changers,
and scattered them this way and that,
and overturned their tables, and threw them down.
And to those selling doves he said:
Take these things hence, out of this place,
and do not make my Father's house, which is a house of prayer, a house of trade and bargaining.
behold: Behold the merchants seated in the temple court,
the place where men came to pray and the teachers taught;
oxen and sheep and doves set out for sale,
and the money-changers at their tables, counting their gain.
Behold the priests, who out of greed by their many inventions stripped the people bare,
who set these sellers there so the pilgrims should have no excuse to leave their offerings undone.
Behold the Lord Jesus make a whip out of little cords,
and drive them all out together — sellers, changers, sheep, and oxen.
See the coins poured out and scattered this way and that,
the tables overturned and thrown down.
Hear him say to those who sold the doves:
Take these things hence; do not make my Father's house a house of trade.
He made the whip from cords: for, as Augustine says,
from our own sins he takes the matter with which he punishes us.
For the dragging-out of sins, sin heaped upon sin, these are the cords;
and Scripture says that the wicked man is bound fast by the ropes of his own sins.
consider: The doves are sold by those who, having received the grace of the Holy Spirit, give it not freely but for a price —
if not for money, then for the favor of the crowd;
not for life's true merit, but for human praise.
The sheep are sold when works of purity and mercy are done for human praise, under hypocrisy, in sheep's clothing —
for the hypocrites have a wolf's mind beneath the wool of the sheep.
The oxen that plough are the preachers of heavenly doctrine,
and they are sold by those who preach not for love of God, but for worldly gain.
All these Christ cast out of the temple,
showing that they are shut out of the temple of God's holy glory.
You who would not be carried off out of the heavenly house when the Lord comes,
carry these things out of your own acts.
For now they are scourged with the cords of their sins for correction;
and the uncorrected, in the end, will be bound fast for damnation.
Let each Christian among Christ's members be consumed with zeal for the house of God.
Who is consumed with zeal for God's house? He who, whatever he sees gone wrong, strives to set it right, longs to mend it, and will not rest.
If you cannot amend it — bear it, groan, sigh, endure the chaff.
Do you see a brother running to the spectacle? Forbid him, warn him, grieve. So the zeal of the Lord's house consumes you.
But if you grow cold and withered, content with yourself alone, and say in your heart: What is it to me to care for the sins of others? My own soul is enough —
does that servant not come to your mind, who hid his talent and would not spend it?
For was he not accused, not because he lost it, but because he kept it without gain?
Do not cease to win souls for Christ, for you yourselves were won by Christ.
prayer: Lord Jesus, let the zeal of your house consume me.
Make a whip even of my own sins,
and drive out from the temple of my heart all that I keep there for sale —
the doves I would not give freely,
the sheep's clothing over a wolf's mind,
the works I do for human praise and not for love of you.
Pour out and scatter my buying and bargaining,
overturn the tables where I count my gain,
and do not let my soul, which should be a house of prayer, become a house of trade.
Bind me now with the cords of correction,
that I be not bound in the end for damnation;
and let me not rest, but labor to win for you the souls you have won.

latinJewel.english: The zeal of your house has consumed me.
